text: July 2 in the History of Computer Science: the BitTorrent protocol is introduced, enabling
users to share files not from a central server, but directly with each
other. On this day in 2001, Bram Cohen released BitTorrent and announced it to
the world. This decentralized approach made large-scale file distribution efficient and accessible, influencing
not just open-source projects but the design of peer-to-peer systems for decades to come.
